programmes.show_description: Round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: The life of Jeffrey Rignall, one of Gacy's few surviving victims, is chronicled. The killer's execution day causes massive protests and reunions as victims' family members and those who worked the case seek closure, and Amirante tries to move on.
James is joined by double Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, chefs Meliz Berg and John Hooker, and First Dates barman Merlin Griffiths

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by A Place in the Sun presenter and musician Leah Charles-King for cooking, chat and craft. Artist Marion Sidebottom shows how to make pictures using the sun, while there is an animal toy-making challenge at the wildlife park, and upcycler Max McMurdo makes display shelving from old plastic pipes
A round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are Tessa Peake-Jones, Adam Garcia and Phil Daniels, while garden designer Chris Hull has the latest on multi-stem trees. Ian Burrell gives a masterclass in no-frills rum, and there is a visit to a farm that is helping adults with disabilities and autism through nature

Winter delivers a blanket of snow across the Peak District, so Liz and Kelvin brave the elements to check on their livestock and make sure they're all well fed and watered. Marnie and Milo also get stuck into jobs around the farm and find fun in even the muckiest of jobs, while after a day of graft the whole family is rewarded with sledging fun

Alan Titchmarsh and the garden makeover team head to Chippenham, Wiltshire to create a traditional formal garden for an inspirational couple. Richard Warren has been a serving Special Constable for 52 years, and dedicates his spare time to running marathons to raise money for charity, and caring for his wife Lynette, who has multiple sclerosis. The team shares tips on how to create a formal pond from scratch, screening solutions for unsightly spots in the garden, and reveals how to mark out a perfectly oval lawn
Round-up of the latest headlines

Ant and Dec host as the auditions return to Blackpool's Winter Gardens, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of today's performers be just what the pair are looking for?

Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in a fake love-themed show hosted by Matt and Emma Willis, who are also in on the whole thing and even get set their own mission in a hot tub. Plus, Olivia Attwood is the guest saboteur
Bradley Walsh hosts and is behind Karen from Dumfries, Ben from Oldham, Andy from Leeds and Sam from Dagenham all the way as they try to beat one of the country's finest quizzers.
Round-up of the latest headlines
Cain is determined, while Matty delivers devastating news and Tracy is feeling heartbroken
Todd's friends discuss their evidence against Theo, Mal calls at Number 7 to deliver a bombshell to Dev, and Ollie urges Will to stop covering for Megan

The aftermath of ACC Cassian Pewe's betrayal hangs heavily over East Sussex Police several months on as the team is in the midst of an anti-corruption enquiry while Roy wrestles with whether to trust Cassian's intel about Sandy's murder and the threat to Bruno's life. Meanwhile, his team is plunged into a case filled with secrets after a Brighton property developer reports his wife missing. Return of the crime drama, starring John Simm
Round-up of the latest headlines

Graham Norton hosts the game show as barrister Daniel, site manager Lorraine and software sales rep Nicole compete to solve word puzzles to get the chance to spin the wheel and win big money prizes, with a potential jackpot of £50,000
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Winter delivers a blanket of snow across the Peak District, so Liz and Kelvin brave the elements to check on their livestock and make sure they're all well fed and watered. Marnie and Milo also get stuck into jobs around the farm and find fun in even the muckiest of jobs, while after a day of graft the whole family is rewarded with sledging fun
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Richard Blackwood, Julia Bradbury and Roman Kemp to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice
The game of words hosted by Adil Ray - trying to Lingo all the way against the reigning Lingo champions are mother and daughter Amanda and Emily and brothers Martyn and Stuart.
Stephen Mulhern hosts as a brave contestant takes on the infamous Banker in the exciting and iconic gameshow and could be in with the chance of winning a life-changing cash prize.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh hosts the show where thousands of pounds are at stake. All that stands between James from St Ives, Rebecca from Stafford, Huw from Salisbury, Judith from Monmouthshire and that money is the small matter of one of the country's finest quiz brains
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

The farm opens up to the public for an Easter event that is Liz and Kelvin's chance to bring in more income, while there is drama in the lambing shed when a vet attends to a ewe struggling to give birth. It's time to train the cows and get them used to being on head collars as Kelvin enters them into the Royal Cheshire Show, and Marnie and Milo learn about the important role of hedgerows on the farm
Kim may have been poisoned, while Paddy makes a mess of things and Graham's suspicions are aroused

programmes.show_name: I'm a Celebrity South Africa: Sneak Peek
programmes.show_description: A preview of the second series of the programme in which memorable campmates from past series of I'm A Celebrity...Get Me Out Of Here! compete against each other in challenges that are bigger and tougher than before - and more unforgiving
Ben's situation forces Tim to question his past, Gary sends Theo packing, George provides Todd with the means to make a quick getaway, and Bethany shows Jodie a picture of her date
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories
